title: How Different from the Past? Spatio-Temporal Time Series Forecasting with Self-Supervised Deviation Learning (ST-SSDL)
AutoSTF: Decoupled Neural Architecture Search for Cost-Effective Automated Spatio-Temporal Forecasting
STD-MAE: Spatial-Temporal Dual Masked Autoencoder for Traffic Forecasting
abstract: Applies masked autoencoder paradigm to spatial-temporal traffic data. Pre-trains with dual masking strategy (masking both spatial nodes and temporal steps) to learn robust representations for downstream forecasting.
notes: IJCAI 2024. Brings self-supervised learning (MAE) to traffic forecasting. Growing trend toward pre-training approaches.

title: Efficient Large-Scale Traffic Forecasting with Transformers: A Spatial Data Management Perspective

metrics.Note: PatchSTG uses 'Spatial Patching' to partition large-scale road networks into non-overlapping subgraphs, reducing the quadratic complexity of attention to linear, achieving 2x-5x training speedup compared to baselines like D2STGNN and ST-Mixer.

title: FlashST: A Simple and Universal Prompt-Tuning Framework for Traffic Prediction

metrics.Note: FlashST employs a lightweight spatio-temporal prompt network and distribution mapping to adapt pre-trained models to downstream tasks. It reduces training time by 20-80% compared to full fine-tuning while significantly improving generalization on distribution-shifted data.

title: ConvTimeNet: A Deep Hierarchical Fully Convolutional Model for Multivariate Time Series Analysis

metrics.Note: Designed as a deep hierarchical fully convolutional network that captures both global and local temporal patterns effectively, offering a strong alternative to Transformer-based models.

title: Fredformer: Frequency Debiased Transformer for Time Series Forecasting

metrics.Note: Addresses the frequency bias issue in Transformers by incorporating a frequency-debiased attention mechanism, improving performance on datasets with complex periodic patterns.

title: Pathformer: Multi-scale Transformers with Adaptive Pathways for Time Series Forecasting

metrics.Note: Proposed a multi-scale transformer with adaptive pathways to capture temporal dynamics at different resolutions, outperforming PatchTST on several long-term forecasting tasks.

title: A novel hybrid time-varying graph neural network for traffic flow forecasting

metrics.Note: Features a time-aware multi-head attention mechanism and a coupled graph learning mechanism to capture dynamic spatial correlations and long-term dependencies.

title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers

metrics.Note: Introduces 'Patching' and 'Channel Independence' to Transformers, significantly improving long-term forecasting accuracy and efficiency (O(L) complexity).

title: Make Graph Neural Networks Great Again: A Generic Integration Paradigm of Topology-Free Patterns for Traffic Speed Prediction

metrics.Note: Proposes a distillation-style learning framework where a GNN 'teacher' injects topology-regularized patterns into a Dual Cross-Scale Transformer (DCST) 'student' to capture topology-free dynamics.

title: Spatial-Temporal Large Language Model for Traffic Prediction

metrics.Note: Treats traffic prediction as af language modeling task by defining timesteps as tokens and using a partially frozen attention strategy to adapt LLMs for spatial-temporal dependencies.

title: T-Graphormer: Using Transformers for Spatiotemporal Forecasting

metrics.Note: Enhances the Graphormer architecture with temporal encodings, allowing each node to attend to all other tokens in the graph sequence to capture rich spacetime patterns.

title: Context-aware knowledge graph framework for traffic speed forecasting using graph neural network

metrics.Note: Integrates a Context-aware Knowledge Graph (CKG) with GNNs using a relation-dependent integration strategy to model urban context dependencies.

title: EasyST: A Simple Framework for Spatio-Temporal Prediction

metrics.Note: EasyST uses a teacher-student distillation framework where a lightweight MLP (student) learns from a complex GNN (teacher), achieving comparable accuracy with significantly lower inference latency (up to 12x faster).

title: Embracing Large Language Models in Traffic Flow Forecasting

metrics.Note: LEAF utilizes LLMs as a 'selector' to choose the best prediction from a dual-branch (Graph/Hypergraph) predictor, enhancing adaptability to distribution shifts.

title: Meta Dynamic Graph for Traffic Flow Prediction

metrics.Note: MetaDG generates dynamic adjacency matrices and meta-parameters at each time step to explicitly model spatio-temporal dynamics and heterogeneity simultaneously.

title: Bridging Traffic State and Trajectory for Dynamic Road Network and Trajectory Representation Learning

metrics.Note: Proposes a framework to bridge macroscopic traffic states and microscopic vehicle trajectories using a co-attentional Transformer.

title: Multi-Source Urban Traffic Flow Forecasting with Drone and Loop Detector Data

metrics.Note: Demonstrates that integrating drone data improves prediction accuracy in scenarios with low sensor coverage and noise.

title: Dynamic Trend Fusion Module for Traffic Flow Prediction

metrics.Note: Consistently reduces prediction error compared to 18 baselines by fusing dynamic trends and static graph structures.

title: Decomposed Spatio-Temporal Mamba for Long-Term Traffic Prediction

metrics.Note: Replaces Transformer attention with Mamba (SSM), reducing complexity from quadratic O(L^2) to linear O(L). Achieves ~60% speedup in inference.

title: BigST: Linear Complexity Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network for Traffic Forecasting on Large-Scale Road Networks

metrics.Note: Achieves linear complexity O(N), significantly faster training/inference on large-scale graphs compared to standard GNNs.

title: Irregular Traffic Time Series Forecasting Based on Asynchronous Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks

Performance.MAE: Improved over standard GCNs on irregular grids
Performance.Handling: Native support for asynchronous/missing time steps

metrics.Note: Specifically designed for irregular sampling intervals and asynchronous traffic data collection.

title: STHSepNet: Spatio-Temporal Hypergraph Separation Network for Traffic Flow Demand Forecasting
abstract: Uses hypergraph representations for modeling higher-order spatial relationships in traffic flow demand forecasting.

title: STWave: When Spatio-Temporal Meet Wavelets - Disentangled Traffic Forecasting via Efficient Spectral Graph Attention Networks
abstract: Combines wavelets with ST prediction for multi-scale temporal pattern analysis in traffic forecasting.

title: Riding the Wave: Multi-Scale Spatial-Temporal Graph Learning
abstract: Addresses multi-scale ST graph learning for capturing patterns at multiple temporal and spatial resolutions.

title: UniST: A Prompt-Empowered Universal Model for Urban Spatio-Temporal Prediction

metrics.Note: Achieves state-of-the-art performance by using masked spatio-temporal pre-training.

title: DSTAGNN: Dynamic Spatial-Temporal Aware Graph Neural Network for Traffic Flow Forecasting
key_contributions: Captures time-varying correlations at each time step
Significant improvements over static graph methods
abstract: Proposes dynamic ST-aware GNN with learnable graph structure at each time step. Captures both static topology and dynamic correlations. Strong performance but LargeST reveals scalability limitations on large graphs.
notes: ICML 2022. Strong performance on medium datasets. LargeST benchmark shows it cannot scale to GLA/CA due to memory requirements from dynamic graph generation.

title: Information Bottleneck-guided MLPs for Robust Spatial-temporal Forecasting
LSTTN: A Long-Short Term Transformer-based Spatio-temporal Neural Network for Traffic Flow Forecasting
key_contributions: Masked subseries Transformer to learn compressed contextual temporal representations from long historical series
Long-term trend extractor using stacked 1D dilated convolutions
Periodicity extractor using dynamic graph convolution for weekly and daily patterns
Short-term trend extractor using existing STGNNs for fine-grained predictions
Integrates long-term trends, periodic features, and short-term features comprehensively
abstract: LSTTN addresses limitations of existing STGNNs that only utilize short-range traffic data by comprehensively considering long- and short-term features. Uses masked subseries Transformer for efficient learning of compressed temporal representations from long series, extracts long-term trends and periodic features, then fuses with short-term features from STGNNs for accurate predictions.
notes: Published in Knowledge-Based Systems 2024 (293: 111637). Central South University and Xiangjiang Laboratory. Uses two-week historical data (4032 timesteps for 5-min intervals). Mask reconstruction with 75% random masking. Graph WaveNet adopted as short-term trend extractor. Demonstrates robustness to missing data and superior sensing of sudden traffic changes.

title: Efficient Traffic Prediction Through Spatio-Temporal Distillation
Taming the Long Tail in Human Mobility Prediction
methodology: Long-tail Distribution Learning, Human Mobility, Domain Adaptation
notes: Addresses long-tail distribution problem in human mobility prediction.

title: CoMaPOI: Multi-Agent Framework for Next POI Prediction
methodology: Multi-agent Systems, POI Prediction, Collaborative Learning
notes: SIGIR 2025. Multi-agent approach for POI prediction.

title: RNTrajRec: Road Network Enhanced Trajectory Recovery with Spatial-Temporal Transformer

Performance.Recovery Rate: High accuracy on low-sampling rate data

metrics.Note: Combines a GridGNN for road segment embedding and a Spatial-Temporal Transformer to recover high-resolution trajectories from sparse GPS points.

title: GETNext: Trajectory Flow Map Enhanced Transformer for Next POI Recommendation
Beyond Regularity: Modeling Chaotic Mobility Patterns for Next Location Prediction

metrics.Note: Proposes CANOE, a model with Chaotic Neural Oscillatory Attention to handle the dynamic imbalance between periodic and chaotic mobility patterns.

title: Timestamps as Prompts for Geography-Aware Location Recommendation

Performance.Recall@5: Significant improvement over STAN and GeoSAN

metrics.Note: TPG introduces a temporal prompt mechanism to enable flexible interval prediction (predicting visits at arbitrary future times) and uses a shifted window mechanism to handle the hard boundary problem in geographic gridding.

title: ROTAN: A Rotation-based Temporal Attention Network for Time-Specific Next POI Recommendation

metrics.Note: ROTAN models continuous time information using rotation operations in a complex vector space to capture time-specific user preferences.

title: CLSPRec: Contrastive Learning of Long and Short-term Preferences for Next POI Recommendation

metrics.Note: Uses contrastive learning to align and integrate long-term and short-term user preferences, addressing data sparsity and preference drift.

title: Adaptive Graph Representation Learning for Next POI Recommendation

metrics.Note: Proposes an Adaptive Graph Representation Learning Network (AGRAN) to capture collaborative signals and transition patterns dynamically.

title: Personalized Long- and Short-term Preference Learning for Next POI Recommendation

metrics.Note: Separately models long-term and short-term preferences and fuses them to predict the next visit. Published in TKDE (early access 2020, issue 2022).

title: LightPath: Lightweight and Scalable Path Representation Learning

metrics.Note: Outperforms supervised baselines like HierETA while being unsupervised and lightweight.

title: More Than Routing: Joint GPS and Route Modeling for Refine Trajectory Representation Learning

metrics.Note: JGRM proposes a joint GPS and Route modeling framework for trajectory representation learning, utilizing a hierarchical GPS encoder and a route encoder with self-supervised tasks (masked language modeling and contrastive learning) to capture fine-grained motion details and road topology.

title: Improving Transferability for Cross-domain Trajectory Prediction via Neural Stochastic Differential Equation

metrics.Note: TrajSDE utilizes continuous and stochastic representations of Neural SDEs to alleviate discrepancies caused by different data acquisition strategies, significantly improving transferability across different autonomous driving datasets.

title: Disentangled Contrastive Hypergraph Learning for Next POI Recommendation

metrics.Note: DCHL constructs three distinct hypergraphs (collaborative, transitional, geographical) and employs a multi-view disentangled hypergraph learning component with cross-view contrastive learning to capture complex user-POI interactions.

title: Generative Next POI Recommendation with Semantic ID

NYC.Note: Uses RQ-VAE to quantize POI coordinates into Semantic IDs for LLM generation.

title: PLMTrajRec: A Scalable and Generalizable Trajectory Recovery Method with Pre-trained Language Models

Performance.Improvement: Significantly outperforms interpolation and RNN baselines

metrics.Note: Converts trajectory recovery into a language modeling task using dual prompts (Explicit & Implicit).

title: Origin-Destination Travel Time Oracle for Map-based Services

metrics.Note: DOT uses a diffusion-based approach to infer 'pixelated trajectories' (PiT) first, then estimates time, outperforming end-to-end regression models.

title: Fine-Grained Trajectory-based Travel Time Estimation for Multi-city Scenarios Based on Deep Meta-Learning

metrics.Note: Uses a meta-learning based framework (MetaTTE) to transfer knowledge from data-rich cities to data-scarce cities for travel time estimation.

title: Multi View Spatial-Temporal Model for Travel Time Estimation

metrics.Note: MVSTM captures spatial, trajectory, and semantic views using Graph2Vec, dual-channel temporal modules, and structural embeddings to estimate travel time.

title: DutyTTE: Deciphering Uncertainty in Origin-Destination Travel Time Estimation

metrics.Note: Proposes a Reinforcement Learning method to align predicted paths with ground truth and a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) for uncertainty.

title: TTPNet: A Neural Network for Travel Time Prediction Based on Tensor Decomposition and Graph Embedding

metrics.Note: Integrates tensor decomposition for speed pattern recovery and graph embedding for road structure.

title: When Will We Arrive? A Novel Multi-Task Spatio-Temporal Attention Network Based on Individual Preference for Estimating Travel Time

metrics.Note: Explicitly models 'Individual Preference' and 'Traffic Prediction' as auxiliary tasks to improve ETA accuracy.

title: Multi-Faceted Route Representation Learning for Travel Time Estimation

metrics.Note: Decomposes routes into three sequences: trajectory (GPS), attribute (road features), and semantic (IDs), fusing them for robust estimation.

title: Multimodal Trajectory Representation Learning for Travel Time Estimation

metrics.Note: MDTI integrates GPS sequences, grid trajectories, and road network constraints using a multimodal fusion module to enhance trajectory representation for TTE.

title: Link Representation Learning for Probabilistic Travel Time Estimation

metrics.Note: Models the joint distribution of travel times across multiple trips using learnable link representations to capture inter-trip and intra-trip correlations.

title: Interpreting Trajectories from Multiple Views: A Hierarchical Self-Attention Network for Estimating the Time of Arrival

metrics.Note: HierETA employs a hierarchical self-attention network to model trajectories from three views: segment (local traffic), link (common patterns), and intersection (indirect influence), achieving superior interpretability and accuracy over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: HetETA: Heterogeneous Information Network Embedding for Estimating Time of Arrival

metrics.Note: HetETA constructs a Heterogeneous Information Network (HIN) from the road map and vehicle trajectories, utilizing graph neural networks to learn representations of spatio-temporal heterogeneous information (recent, daily, and weekly periods) for accurate ETA prediction.

title: DeepMM: Deep Learning Based Map Matching with Data Augmentation

metrics.Note: Uses a seq2seq architecture with attention to map sparse and noisy trajectories to road segments in a latent space, augmented by a data generation mechanism.

title: GraphMM: Graph-Based Vehicular Map Matching by Leveraging Trajectory and Road Correlations

metrics.Note: GraphMM leverages Graph Neural Networks (GNN) to explicitly model the topology correlations between the trajectory graph and the road network graph for accurate matching.

title: DiffMM: Efficient Method for Accurate Noisy and Sparse Trajectory Map Matching via One Step Diffusion

metrics.Note: DiffMM is the first to model map matching through a conditional diffusion paradigm. It uses a one-step diffusion process for efficiency, significantly outperforming HMM and previous Deep Learning methods on sparse/noisy data.

title: Efficient Methods for Accurate Sparse Trajectory Recovery and Map Matching

metrics.F1-Score: Consistently achieves best result quality compared to baselines.
metrics.Speed: Orders of magnitude faster than competitors during training and inference.

note: Proposes a two-stage framework: MMA (Map Matching) formulates point-to-segment matching as a classification task over a small candidate set, and TRMMA (Trajectory Recovery) uses a dual-transformer to infer missing points along the matched route.

title: L2MM: Learning to Map Matching with Deep Models for Low-Quality GPS Trajectory Data
conference: ACM TKDD (Transactions on Knowledge Discovery from Data)

metrics.Robustness: Handles noisy, low-frequency, and non-uniform data effectively

note: L2MM formulates map matching as a sequence-to-sequence translation task using a Transformer-based encoder-decoder architecture to capture trajectory behaviors and spatial dependencies.

title: RLOMM: An Efficient and Robust Online Map Matching Framework with Reinforcement Learning

Performance.Accuracy: Significantly higher than SOTA online methods
Performance.Efficiency: High throughput suitable for online applications

metrics.Note: Models online map matching as an Online Markov Decision Process (OMDP) and uses Deep Q-Learning with a comprehensive reward function (accuracy, continuity, detour penalty) to make future-oriented matching decisions.
